title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After investigating Giuseppe’s employment history, Anthony discovers that the Italian had been present during major thefts at two of his former hotels. Anthony suspects Giuseppe was not merely a thief but was sent by someone, noting that Giuseppe’s intense reaction to a letter on the table suggested a specific motive beyond simple theft. Despite Anthony's efforts to find Giuseppe through employee inquiries and newspaper advertisements, he finds no leads.

Anthony receives a phone call from Mr. Pottsford of Puddington Haggis Publishing. Pottsford warns that the manuscript Anthony possesses is being targeted by a dangerous group using bribery and threats. To ensure safety, Pottsford arranges for a company representative, Mr. Holmes, to collect the manuscript from Anthony's hotel and provide a £1,000 check. Following Pottsford's advice, Anthony places a fake manuscript in the hotel safe to mislead potential enemies. The following morning, Anthony hands the manuscript to Mr. Holmes and receives the payment.

After checking out of the hotel, Anthony receives a cryptic, formal letter regarding the memoirs of the Earl of Stapleton. The document requests that he consult with Mr. George Romax and invites him to visit the Chimney House as a guest of the Earl of Chatham on Friday. To protect his identity, Anthony checks into a secluded inn under the alias Anthony Kidd. He writes a letter to the Earl of Carringham, signed "Jimmy McGregor," stating he has already delivered the manuscript to the publishers and is leaving Britain. Meanwhile, Virginia Rue prepares to encounter a blackmailer by changing her persona from a simple-minded fool to someone more alert.
